description: In a temperate four-season cycle, 'muero' comes between summer and winter. Cooling air, shortening days, harvest, and falling leaves may mark it, though local ecologies do not all show those changes in the same way.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm' and let air pass through the nose into high-back rounded 'u'. Unround and bring the tongue forward for the separate stressed middle-height 'e'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge, then finish with middle-height rounded 'o'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Autumn names a season in which harvesting, storing, and preparation for colder weather have organised practical work in many temperate communities.

semantic_domains.nature: the cooling temperate season associated with harvest and leaf fall
semantic_domains.temporal: the season between summer and winter
